raw_consumer_statement:
REBUTTAL TO CITI RESPONSE : FORMAL ASSERTION OF CLAIMS AND DEFENSES Case # XXXX I am formally rebutting Citis denial dated XX/XX/year>. Citibank is incorrectly attempting to adjudicate this matter under 12 CFR 1026.13 ( Billing Errors ) and internal XXXX timeframes. I am not reporting a billing error ; I am formally asserting my rights under 12 CFR 1026.12 ( c ) ( Claims and Defenses ). 

Under federal law, a consumers right to assert claims and defenses against a card issuer for defective or misrepresented goods is not subject to the 60-day billing error window or XXXX  120-day rule. This right exists for up to one year from the date of the first statement, provided the purchase exceeds {$50.00} and occurred in the consumer 's home state. 

1. Fact : Material Misrepresentation & Breach of Warranty Jackerys marketing ( Exhibit B ) and technical manuals ( Exhibit C ) explicitly promise " XXXX XXXX '' ( retention of 95 % charge for 1 year ) and a " 30-day backup '' capability. 

However, in the attached correspondence ( Exhibit A ), XXXX XXXX XXXX admits the unit has a " normal '' idle drain of XXXX per hour. 

The Mathematical Proof of Fraud : A XXXX hourly drain equals XXXX per day. On a XXXX unit, the battery is depleted in XXXX days while doing nothing. 

The Violation : This represents a 9,000 % deviation from the XXXX standby capability advertised. This XXXX " normal '' drain is documented in writing by the merchant but is undisclosed on their website and in their manual. 

XXXX. Justification for Total Recovery of {$12000.00} Citi is legally obligated to make the consumer whole. This total includes the {$2000.00} installation fee. Because the hardware is fundamentally defective and incapable of performing the " whole-home backup '' function as marketed, the professional installation is valueless. I have relied on the merchants marketing and their recommended installers to my financial detriment. 

3. Final Assertion I made a good-faith effort to resolve this with XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX following the XX/XX/XXXX professional installation. The merchants refusal to honor a refund for a product that fails to meet its own basic technical specifications is the exact scenario 12 CFR 1026.12 ( c ) was enacted to prevent. 

I expect Citibank to comply with federal regulations and immediately reverse the charges for this defective equipment and the associated installation costs.
